guide.name: Getting started
guide.description: Install the Acme design system, theme your app, and render your first component.

items.title: Overview
items.body: The Acme design system is a shared library of components, tokens, and patterns. This guide takes you from an empty project to a themed app with a working component in three steps.

items.title: Prerequisites
items.body: You need Node.js 18 or later and a React 18 project. No design tooling is required to follow this guide.

items.title: Install the package
items.instruction: Add the UI package to your project.

items.title: Wrap your app in the provider
items.instruction: Mount the `ThemeProvider` at the root so every component inherits the theme.
items.expectedResult: Components render with Acme's default light theme.

items.title: Render your first component
items.instruction: Drop a `Button` anywhere inside the provider.
items.expectedResult: A themed primary button renders and responds to hover and focus.

items.guidance: Import components from the package root, not from deep internal paths.
items.rationale: Deep imports bypass the public API and break when internal file structure changes.

items.guidance: Render every Acme component inside a single ThemeProvider.
items.rationale: Components read theme values from context; outside the provider they fall back to unstyled defaults.

items.description: Starting a new Acme product or prototype from scratch.
Migrating an existing app from v1 — follow the migration guide instead.

agentDocumentBlocks.purpose: Guide a developer through installing the design system and rendering a first themed component.

items.description: Getting started is for new projects; the migration guide is for upgrading an existing app between major versions.

items.guidance: Always wrap the app in ThemeProvider before using any component.
Do not import from deep internal package paths.
